chapter: Chapter 8: Measurement of Time and Motion

questions.question: Define motion and give examples from daily life.
questions.answer: Motion is the change in position of an object with respect to time. Examples: a moving car, a flying bird, a rolling ball.

questions.question: Differentiate between distance and displacement with examples.
questions.answer: Distance: total path travelled, scalar quantity (e.g., 5 km). Displacement: shortest path between start and end, vector quantity (e.g., 3 km east).

questions.question: Define speed and calculate average speed of a car travelling 150 km in 3 hours.
questions.answer: Speed = distance/time = 150/3 = 50 km/h.

questions.question: Explain uniform motion and give an example.
questions.answer: Uniform motion is motion in which a body covers equal distances in equal intervals of time. Example: a train moving at a constant speed of 60 km/h.

questions.question: A car travels 60 km north in 1 hour and 40 km south in 0.5 hours. Find average speed and velocity.
questions.answer: Total distance = 60+40=100 km; total time =1+0.5=1.5 h; average speed =100/1.5 ≈66.67 km/h. Displacement = 60-40=20 km north; average velocity=20/1.5≈13.33 km/h north.

questions.question: Explain velocity and acceleration with examples.
questions.answer: Velocity = displacement/time (vector), example: 50 km/h east. Acceleration = change in velocity/time, example: car speeds up from 0 to 20 m/s in 5 s → a=4 m/s².

questions.question: A bike moves at 20 m/s for 10 s. Calculate distance covered.
questions.answer: Distance = speed × time = 20 ×10=200 m.

questions.question: Define scalar and vector quantities with examples from motion.
questions.answer: Scalar: only magnitude (e.g., distance, speed). Vector: magnitude + direction (e.g., displacement, velocity).

questions.question: Explain non-uniform motion with an example.
questions.answer: Non-uniform motion: motion in which speed changes with time. Example: a car accelerating from rest to 60 km/h.

questions.question: A car accelerates from 10 m/s to 30 m/s in 5 s. Find acceleration.
questions.answer: Acceleration = (v-u)/t = (30-10)/5 = 4 m/s².

questions.question: Explain distance-time and velocity-time graphs with examples.
questions.answer: Distance-time: slope = speed, straight line → uniform motion, curved → acceleration. Velocity-time: slope = acceleration, area under graph = displacement.

questions.question: Derive s=ut+½at² from v = u + at.
questions.answer: From v = u + at → t = (v-u)/a. Substitute in s=½(u+v)t → s = ut + ½at².

questions.question: A ball is dropped from 20 m. Find time to reach the ground (g=10 m/s²).
questions.answer: s=½gt² → 20=5t² → t=2 s.

questions.question: Explain the difference between instantaneous speed and average speed.
questions.answer: Instantaneous: speed at a specific moment (speedometer reading). Average: total distance / total time. Example: 100 km in 2 h → average speed = 50 km/h.

questions.question: Calculate distance covered by a car moving at 15 m/s for 8 s.
questions.answer: Distance = speed × time = 15 × 8 = 120 m.

questions.question: A particle starts from rest and accelerates at 2 m/s² for 6 s. Calculate final velocity and distance travelled.
Explain how to measure time using a stopwatch and pendulum.
questions.answer: Stopwatch: start/stop to measure intervals. Pendulum: T=2π√(l/g); e.g., l=1 m → T≈2 s. Total time = n×T.

questions.question: A car travels 40 km north in 30 min and 60 km south in 1 h. Find average speed and velocity.
questions.answer: Total distance = 40+60=100 km, total time=1.5 h, average speed=66.67 km/h. Displacement=40-60=-20 km south, average velocity= -20/1.5≈13.33 km/h south.

questions.question: Explain motion along a straight line using equations of motion.
questions.answer: Use v = u + at, s = ut + ½ at², v² = u² + 2as to calculate displacement, velocity, or acceleration for linear motion.

questions.question: A particle moves with velocity v=4t m/s. Find distance covered in 5 s.
Explain uniform and non-uniform acceleration with examples.
questions.answer: Uniform acceleration: constant rate of change of velocity (e.g., freely falling object). Non-uniform: varying rate of change (e.g., a car slowing down).

questions.question: A ball is thrown vertically upwards with speed 20 m/s. Calculate time to reach maximum height (g=10 m/s²).
questions.answer: At max height, v=0 → t=v/g=20/10=2 s.

questions.question: Explain difference between distance-time and velocity-time graphs.
questions.answer: Distance-time: slope = speed, straight line → uniform, curved → acceleration. Velocity-time: slope = acceleration, area = displacement.

questions.question: A particle starts from rest, accelerates at 3 m/s² for 5 s, then moves at constant speed. Calculate total distance in 10 s.
questions.answer: Distance during acceleration: s1=0.5×3×25=37.5 m; velocity after acceleration v=15 m/s; distance at constant speed s2=15×5=75 m; total distance = 37.5+75=112.5 m.

questions.question: Explain measurement of time using atomic clocks.
questions.answer: Atomic clock measures vibrations of atoms (like cesium) to provide highly precise time. Used in GPS and scientific experiments.
